engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is deciding whether to trust a named practitioner (Dott. Ernest Schoenmaker) and his 36-year clinic — the page leads with credentials, professional memberships, philosophy, insurance reimbursement and a testimonial. That is selling defined expertise, so trust wins on the first rung.

goal.one_action: book an appointment (or phone the clinic)

structure.archetype_note: Multi-page local clinic site: rich home overview + about/team, condition & service detail pages, blog, and a contact/hours/map page. Conversion (booking) is handed off to external Cituro system.

mapped.observed_as: Over Thirty Years of Experience in Physiotherapy and Natural Therapies
Specialized in Alternative Medicine and Complementary Care
Our Care is Reimbursable Through Health Insurance
Our Objective Is To Help You Feel Better Naturally

unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical id for a local-business opening-hours block; folded into miscellaneous but it is really a map-hours capability signal

proposed_new.name: external-booking-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: Primary 'Prenota Un Appuntamento' CTA hands off to the external Cituro online booking system; no on-site scheduling flow

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full; clear trust archetype, credentials, testimonial, and an external booking handoff all legible from the raw capture.

gap_findings: Trilingual (IT/EN/DE) exceeds the closed 'bilingual' capability — vocab has no >2-language expression.
Opening-hours table has no canonical section id (only the map-hours capability captures it).
External booking handoff (Cituro) is captured only via the proposed external-booking-redirect capability, not by any goal-mechanism nuance.
